scope.family: finite stabilizer EPR-pairing resources with named left/right mouth ports
scope.minimality_scope: permutation EPR resources; first fixed-mouth channel split occurs at m=2

core_claim: Coarse L/R entropy and min-cut count EPR resources but do not determine the named bridge channel; algebraic connectivity does for the finite permutation-EPR family.

theorem_style_claims.claim: For a permutation pi of EPR edges L_i--R_pi(i), the declared identity-mouth teleportation channel Q_i,L_i -> R_i has exact qubit capacity equal to the number of fixed points of pi.
For fixed m, all permutation-EPR resources have the same coarse L/R entropy and min-cut shadow, while fixed-mouth channel capacities vary.

The crossed m=2 resource transfers probes to the wrong right-mouth ports under the identity-mouth coupling; OTOC-like detector signals light up the actual wrong mouth; optimal right-port relabeling restores capacity, so the result is port-resolved rather than a loss of entanglement.

proof_obligations.claim: Emit the finite ER=EPR channel benchmark certificate.
Regression check for the Goal 10 certificate.

diagnostic_hierarchy.coarse_entropy_mincut: insufficient for named fixed-mouth channel capacity
diagnostic_hierarchy.port_resolved_entropy: distinguishes the simple EPR pairing witness
diagnostic_hierarchy.algebraic_connectivity_matrix: sufficient for the declared finite channel family

known_vs_new.known_derived: Standard stabilizer EPR teleportation and Clifford Pauli transfer.
known_vs_new.new_programmatic_contribution: Packages ER=EPR as a finite channel benchmark comparing entropy/min-cut shadows, algebraic connectivity, and operational transfer controls.

limitations: This is a stabilizer port benchmark and exact wrong-mouth/permutation-scrambling proxy, not a continuum-gravity theorem, not a generic chaotic many-body traversable-wormhole simulation, and not a separation from full port-resolved entropy data.
next_frontier: Search non-Clifford or tensor-network families where richer entropy/min-cut diagnostics collide while algebraic connectivity predicts channel capacity.
